segments.text: This is the account of Noah’s sons Shem, Ham, and Japheth, who also had sons after the flood.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
The sons of Japheth:
Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshech, and Tiras.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
The sons of Gomer:
Ashkenaz, Riphath, and Togarmah.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
And the sons of Javan:
Elishah, Tarshish, the Kittites, and the Rodanites.[^44]
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: From these, the maritime peoples separated into their territories, according to their languages, by clans within their nations.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
The sons of Ham:
Cush, Mizraim, Put, and Canaan.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
The sons of Cush:
Seba, Havilah, Sabtah,[^45] Raamah, and Sabteca.
And the sons of Raamah:
Sheba and Dedan.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Cush was the father of Nimrod, who began to be a mighty one[^46] on the earth.
He was a mighty hunter before[^47] the LORD; so it is said,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Like Nimrod, a mighty hunter before the LORD.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: His kingdom began in Babylon, Erech, Accad, and Calneh, in the land of Shinar.[^48]
From that land he went forth into Assyria, where he built Nineveh, Rehoboth-Ir, Calah,
and Resen, which is between Nineveh and the great city of Calah.
Mizraim was the father of the Ludites, the Anamites, the Lehabites, the Naphtuhites,
the Pathrusites, the Casluhites (from whom the Philistines came), and the Caphtorites.[^49]
And Canaan was the father of Sidon his firstborn,[^50] and of the Hittites,
the Jebusites, the Amorites, the Girgashites,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text: the Hivites, the Arkites, the Sinites,
segments.speaker: The LORD

segments.text:
the Arvadites, the Zemarites, and the Hamathites.
Later the Canaanite clans were scattered,
and the borders of Canaan extended from Sidon toward Gerar as far as Gaza, and then toward Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah, and Zeboiim, as far as Lasha.
These are the sons of Ham according to their clans, languages, lands, and nations.
And sons were also born to Shem, the older brother of Japheth;[^51] Shem was the forefather of all the sons of Eber.
segments.speaker: the LORD

segments.text:
The sons of Shem:
Elam, Asshur, Arphaxad, Lud, and Aram.
segments.speaker: the Lord

segments.text:
The sons of Aram:
Uz, Hul, Gether, and Mash.[^52]
Arphaxad was the father of Shelah,[^53] and Shelah was the father of Eber.
Two sons were born to Eber: One was named Peleg,[^54] because in his days the earth was divided, and his brother was named Joktan.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And Joktan was the father of Almodad, Sheleph, Hazarmaveth, Jerah,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Hadoram, Uzal, Diklah,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Obal, Abimael, Sheba,
Ophir, Havilah, and Jobab. All these were sons of Joktan.
Their territory extended from Mesha to Sephar, in the eastern hill country.
These are the sons of Shem, according to their clans, languages, lands, and nations.
All these are the clans of Noah’s sons, according to their generations and nations. From these the nations of the earth spread out after the flood.
